key_insight: A lightweight transfer-learning and hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász training strategy makes BiSeNetV2 far more robust for seam segmentation under reflective welding conditions without changing the model architecture.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical real-time perception upgrade for construction welding robots, improving seam detection on shiny metal surfaces while preserving the speed and efficiency needed for deployment.
method: Start from a BiSeNetV2 backbone and apply transfer learning for seam segmentation.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to stabilize learning and improve boundary continuity.
Focus optimization on reflection robustness rather than adding network complexity.
evidence_signal: The method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown in the reported welding datasets and may still require site-specific validation for different metal finishes, lighting conditions, and seam geometries.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
